content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN integers. Find the count of numbers divisible by KKK.


Input Format
The input begins with two positive integers NNN, KKK. The next NNN lines contains one positive integer each denoted by AiA_iAi​.



Output Format
Output a single number denoting how many integers are divisible by KKK.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and K. The next N lines each contain one integer Ai.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the count of numbers among the given Ai that are divisible by K.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Among the seven numbers, 51, 966369, 9 and 999996 are divisible by 3, giving a count of 4.
Numbers divisible by 2 are 2, 4 and 6, so the answer is 3.
Every integer is divisible by 1; the single given number contributes 1 to the count.

solution.approach: For each test case we simply iterate over the N numbers and increase a counter whenever Ai % K == 0. No preprocessing is required because each query is independent. This linear scan is optimal as every element must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) – each integer is read and a constant‑time modulo operation is performed.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are stored regardless of input size.
